Paper Notes — "Attention Is All You Need" (Vaswani et al., 2017): This landmark NLP paper introduced the Transformer architecture, which relies entirely on self-attention mechanisms and dispenses with recurrence and convolution entirely. The multi-head attention mechanism allows the model to jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces at different positions, capturing diverse relationships in parallel. The encoder-decoder structure with positional encodings enables the model to process sequences of arbitrary length while retaining position information. Transformers achieved state-of-the-art results on English-German and English-French translation benchmarks and have since become the foundation of virtually all modern NLP systems.
